lines.en_lit: “If you shall afflict from conjugal relations, “and if you shall take from rival And say that this and that rival Is it written: If you take? “And if you shall take” is written.

And say that this and that are referring to rival One his rivals. And one rivals who come to him from the world similar “if you shall take.” Is it written: If you take and if you afflict? “if you shall afflict and if you shall take” is written.

Rav Pappa said to Abaye: But conjugal relations themselves are called affliction, as it is written: and he lay with her and afflicted her” He said to him: There, afflicted her from different relations,

The Sages taught: It is prohibited to bathe part of the body the whole body. But if one is dirty from mud or excrement, he bathe in his manner, and he not be concerned it is prohibited to part of the body just as the whole body. But if one was sick or if one had scabs on his head he smear in his manner, and he not be concerned
The school of Menashe taught Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel says: A woman rinse one hand in water, and give bread to her child, and she not be concerned
They said about Shammai the Elder that he did not want to feed with one hand, they decreed that he must feed with two hands, What is the reason Abaye said: Due to Shivta,
The Sages taught: One who goes to greet his father or his teacher, or to greet one who is greater than him cross until his neck is in the water, and he not be concerned

A dilemma was raised before them, What a teacher to student? Come hear Rav Yitzḥak bar bar Ḥana said: I saw Ze’iri, who went to Rav Ḥiyya bar Ashi, his student. Rav Ashi said: that Rav Ḥiyya bar Ashi who went to Ze’iri, his teacher.

Rava permitted those right side to pass through the water to guard the fruit Abaye said to Rava: was taught that supports your Guards of fruit cross until their necks are in the water, and they not be concerned

Rav Yosef permitted people Bei Tarbu to cross in the water to come to the lecture on He did not, permit them to go Abaye said to him: If so, you are obstructing them in the future. Some say permitted them to come and permitted them to go Abaye said to him: Granted, to come, that is well. But what is the reason to go So as not to obstruct them in the future.

Rav Yehuda and Rav Shmuel bar Rav Yehuda were standing on the bank of the Pappa River next to the Ḥatzdad crossing, and Rami bar Pappa was standing on the other side He raised his voice to them What is crossing over to come to you to ask a halakha? Rav Yehuda said to him: Rav and Shmuel who both say: One cross provided that he does not remove his hand from under the hem of his cloak. Some say Rav Shmuel bar Rav Yehuda said to him: We learned cross over, provided he does not remove his hand from the hem of his cloak.

Rav Yosef strongly objects to this: And on a weekday is it permitted in such But isn’t it written “He measured a thousand cubits, and he led me through the water; the water was ankle deep” here is permitted to pass through up to the ankles.

“Again he measured a thousand and he led me through the water, the water was knee deep” here is permitted to pass through up the knees. “He measured a thousand and led me through the water up to the waist” here is permitted to pass through up to the waist. From this forward: “And he measured a thousand, a river that I could not pass through”
Abaye said: a river flowing water is different.

One might think could cross by swimming. The verse states: “For the water had swollen into saḥu waters” What “saḥu waters” a boat shiyuta], as sailing [shayta] is called swimming [sayḥa]. I might have thought could pass across in a small boat [burni]. the verse states: “No galley with oars shall go” might have thought he could pass through in a large boat. the verse states: “Neither shall a fishing boat [tzi adir] be able to cross it” From where be inferred Rav Yosef translated A fishing boat [sfinat tzayyadin] will not travel on it and a large ship will not cross it.

Rabbi Yehuda ben Pazi said: Even the Angel of Death does not have permission to pass through it as it is written here: “No galley with oars [ani shayt] can travel” and it was written there: From going to and fro [mishut] the earth

Rabbi Pineḥas said in the name of Rav Huna of Tzippori: The spring that comes forth from inside the Holy of Holies at first and resembles grasshoppers’ antennae Once it reaches the opening of the Sanctuary it becomes as thread of warp; once it reaches the Entrance Hall it becomes as thread of woof, Once it reaches the opening of courtyard it becomes like the mouth of a small jug. This is as we learned Rav Eliezer ben Ya’akov says: Water,
